given: A reviewer compares all VS2 final/current reports and scenario JSON artifacts.
when: The VS3 evidence reconciliation runs.
then: One canonical current status exists; conflicting VS2 claims are rejected, superseded, or reconciled with exact report paths and hashes.
verification: Run VS3 evidence reconciliation; inspect VS2 JSON summaries; lint claim strings.
evidence: Reconciliation JSON, report hashes, rejected/superseded artifact list, final product claim string.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if one canonical status remains and no report can be used to claim broader readiness; FAIL if contradictory PASS/NOT_VERIFIED signals remain unclassified.

given: VS3 contract and matrix are frozen.
when: Documentation verification runs.
then: Markdown and CSV exist, row counts match, and every row has expected behavior, verification, evidence, pass/fail criteria, owner, and initial status.
verification: Run docs verifier and matrix structural checks.
evidence: scripts/verify_sot_docs.sh, matrix row-count output, duplicate-ID check.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if matrix and contract are internally consistent; FAIL on duplicate IDs, missing criteria, or status-bearing contract claims.

given: A VS3 local/dev report is generated.
when: Static report lint runs over changed docs, reports, README, and UI copy.
then: Report distinguishes VS3-L local/dev assurance from VS3-P production/on-prem candidate and cannot overclaim real IdP, live provider, real network, migration, or human acceptance.
verification: Run static report lint over docs, reports, README, and UI copy touched by VS3.
evidence: Overclaim lint report, negative evidence counters, reviewed allowlist if any.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if forbidden overclaim strings are absent or explicitly historical; FAIL if local proof is described as production/on-prem/live readiness.

given: A VS3 verifier or Make target is introduced.
when: The verifier dry-run/list/coverage path is executed.
then: cornerstone scenario verify vs3-onprem-trusted-extension --json emits status, counts, per-row evidence, human rows, and gate metadata.
verification: Execute dry-run/list/coverage path before full implementation; validate JSON schema.
evidence: CLI transcript, JSON schema validation, coverage matrix.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if CLI is native and status-neutral before implementation; FAIL if raw scripts replace CLI parity.

given: CLI, API, UI, worker, and tool runtime receive the same operation.
when: The operation is submitted through each surface.
then: All surfaces derive RequestContext from trusted identity/membership and produce matching context digests and policy outcomes.
verification: Run normalized fixture across all surfaces.
evidence: CLI/API/UI/worker/tool transcripts, context digest, policy decision ID, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if no surface accepts caller-controlled scope; FAIL on digest mismatch or missing audit refs.

given: Caller supplies forged tenant, owner, namespace, role, classification, egress, connector, or tool grant.
when: Caller invokes protected read, write, policy, tool, connector, model, or action operation.
then: Forged fields are ignored or denied before protected DB access, egress, connector call, or tool execution.
verification: Parameterized negative tests for each field and entry point.
evidence: Denied responses, zero protected DB rows touched, zero egress, policy/audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if every forged-authority path is denied or neutralized; FAIL if any forged value influences authority.

given: Membership or activation grant is revoked after a prior allow.
when: The principal retries through cached session, worker, or tool runtime.
then: New requests deny within the documented revocation window, including cached sessions, workers, and tool runtimes.
implementation_area: Membership store; grant revocation; cache invalidation
verification: Allow -> revoke -> retry test across API, CLI, worker, and pack/tool path.
evidence: Before/after decision revisions, cache invalidation record, denial audit, zero post-revocation side effects.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if stale allow cannot produce data access or side effects; FAIL on stale success beyond documented bound.

given: RequestContext is missing, malformed, expired, conflicted, or unresolvable.
when: Protected operation reaches gateway, service, worker, CLI, or tool entry point.
then: Protected operations fail closed with helpful redacted errors and no downstream DB/egress/tool/provider access.
verification: Fault matrix over gateway, service, worker, CLI, and tool entry points.
evidence: Stable exit/status codes, zero downstream counters, sanitized logs, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if no protected boundary is reached; FAIL if any path falls back open.

given: Mission/workspace authority differs from tenant-level membership.
when: A principal uses memory, connector, model, tool, or action across same-tenant scopes.
then: Tenant membership alone is insufficient; mission/workspace policy controls memory, connector, model, tool, and action use.
verification: Same-tenant personal/org/project fixture with allowed and denied operations.
evidence: Policy decisions, zero implicit context use, allowed promotion provenance, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if namespace/workspace/mission scope is enforced above tenant RLS; FAIL on implicit cross-context use.

given: Active VS0/VS1 durable object families are persisted.
when: Schema inventory and create/read tests run.
then: Required tenant, owner, namespace, workspace, classification, provenance, and audit fields exist and are non-null where required.
verification: Schema inventory plus create/read/null-insert tests.
evidence: Schema report, failed-null insert transcript, representative API/CLI payloads.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if every active truth-bearing table is scoped; FAIL on ownerless global truth or nullable required scope.

given: Tenant A and tenant B data exist in every protected table.
when: Application role reads and queries as tenant A.
then: Application role can read only authorized tenant rows; tenant-B data and existence metadata remain hidden from tenant A.
verification: Two-tenant DB integration matrix using real app role.
evidence: SQL transcript, pg_policies inventory, row counts, zero foreign canaries.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if SELECT/count/join/search paths hide foreign data; FAIL on row, count, ID, cursor, or timing/error leak.

given: Application role attempts cross-tenant INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, bulk mutation, and RETURNING.
when: Mutation matrix executes.
then: RLS/WITH CHECK/constraints deny or affect zero unauthorized rows without partial mutation.
verification: Database mutation matrix with rollback assertions.
evidence: SQLSTATE or neutral API error, before/after snapshots, audit/anomaly event.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if unauthorized mutations have no effect; FAIL on partial cross-tenant write or revealing error.

given: Pool, retry, worker, scheduled job, and cancellation paths reuse infrastructure.
when: Parallel tenant tests run with injected failures.
then: Tenant context is transaction-local or otherwise reset; no request contaminates another request or report.
implementation_area: Connection pool; worker envelopes; scoped caches
verification: Pool stress test with alternating tenants and injected errors/timeouts.
evidence: Connection IDs, tenant sequence, reset assertions, parallel report hash stability.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if repeated parallel tests show zero contamination; FAIL on leaked context or false PASS.

given: Migration imports known, missing, ambiguous, invalid, and duplicate ownership rows.
when: Forward migration, failed migration, quarantine, and rollback run.
then: Known rows migrate deterministically; ambiguous rows quarantine or block; rollback is deterministic; no ownerless truth is created.
verification: Forward migration, failed migration, quarantine, rollback, and restored-read tests.
evidence: Migration report, counts, checksums, quarantine reasons, rollback transcript.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if data integrity and scope are preserved; FAIL on silent default tenant assignment or destructive migration.

given: Backup, tenant export, full restore, and filtered restore paths run.
when: Local backup -> restore -> verify suite executes with two tenants.
then: Backup/restore preserves artifacts, evidence, claims, ontology, policy decisions, audit integrity, RLS policies, and tenant boundaries.
verification: Local backup -> restore -> verify suite with two tenants.
evidence: Backup manifest, restored hashes/counts, RLS inventory before/after, audit verify output.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if restored system reproduces evidence and audit safely; FAIL on missing rows, broken policies, or leaked tenant export.

given: Any protected operation constructs policy input.
when: Policy input schema tests run.
then: Policy input schema includes trusted subject, scope, resource, action, classification, mission authority, connector/tool capability, model policy, risk, data scope, and environment.
verification: Contract tests and golden fixtures for every operation family.
evidence: Schema file, valid/invalid fixture results, source-of-attribute map, input digest.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if unknown or caller-authoritative fields fail closed; FAIL on schema drift or unvalidated authority.

given: OPA/Rego evaluates allow, deny, escalate, undefined, malformed, and conflict cases.
when: OPA tests and HTTP decision tests run.
then: PolicyDecision records action, reason codes, safe resolution, bundle revision/hash, decision ID, input digest, tenant/namespace, evidence refs, and audit refs.
verification: opa test, HTTP decision tests, golden JSON validation.
evidence: OPA JSON output, coverage, PolicyDecision fixtures, audit linkage, redaction proof.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if decisions are deterministic and default deny; FAIL on implicit allow or missing reason/audit refs.

given: OPA service, management APIs, and decision APIs are accessed from allowed and disallowed peers.
when: Allowed and denied network/container peers call OPA.
then: OPA is bound to intended interfaces, authenticated/authorized, and management APIs are not anonymously exposed.
verification: Network/container tests from allowed and denied peers.
evidence: OPA config, listening sockets, authorized success, unauthorized denial, audit/status refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if unauthorized access is denied; FAIL on anonymous policy or data API exposure.

given: Bundle update is valid, invalid, incompatible, stale, or rollback-triggering.
when: Bundle lifecycle and rollback tests run.
then: Activation is atomic; invalid bundles fail without replacing last known good; first-start failure fails closed and is visible.
verification: Bundle lifecycle tests and rollback injection.
evidence: Active revision before/after, invalid-bundle error, degraded readiness, policy decisions under failure.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if no invalid policy becomes active; FAIL on stale permissive allow or hidden degraded state.

given: Policy logs include sensitive input or denied data.
when: Decision logging and audit mirror run.
then: Decision logging and audit mirror mask secrets/protected values while preserving correlation.
verification: Secret-canary fixture through allow, deny, and malformed paths.
evidence: Decision log sample, CornerStone audit refs, zero secret scanner findings.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if logs are useful and redacted; FAIL on raw secret or protected payload leak.

given: Tool/runtime has no egress grant and a reachable forbidden sink exists.
when: Runtime attempts outbound connection.
then: Runtime/network boundary blocks connection; forbidden sink records zero requests and zero bytes.
verification: Container/process integration test with sink counters.
evidence: Denied tool result, sink logs, network counters, policy/audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if no packet/request reaches forbidden sink; FAIL if app merely skips call without runtime proof.

given: Tool/workflow has declared ConnectorHub capability, current approval, policy allow, and mock provider destination.
when: It calls local mock provider through governed egress path.
then: Exactly one approved call reaches allowed sink; evidence, connector result, policy, approval, and audit are linked.
verification: End-to-end local controlled sink test.
evidence: One sink request, sanitized metadata, Action/Workflow/Connector result, decision/audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if exactly the declared call occurs; FAIL on missing call, duplicate call, or undeclared call.

given: Destination uses URL normalization variants, alternate ports, methods, redirect hops, or DNS rebinding.
when: Outbound request is attempted.
then: Every normalized destination and redirect hop is re-authorized; denied addresses are never contacted.
verification: Table-driven URL, redirect, fake-DNS, IPv4/IPv6 tests.
evidence: Per-case decisions, DNS transcript, redirect hop decisions, sink logs, zero denied-address contact.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if all bypass variants deny safely; FAIL on broadened allowlist or header leak.

given: Tool attempts direct sockets, proxy env, alternate DNS, WebSocket/FTP/SMTP, subprocess, shell, host filesystem, or env access.
when: Adversarial sandbox suite runs.
then: Undeclared protocols and host access are blocked by sandbox/capability boundary.
verification: Adversarial sandbox suite.
evidence: Zero unauthorized connections/processes/files/env reads, denied capability records, runtime audit.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if no undeclared access succeeds; FAIL on arbitrary host/shell access.

given: Egress controller, proxy, sandbox policy, or runtime policy component is down or misconfigured.
when: Allowed and denied egress cases rerun.
then: Protected capabilities fail closed; readiness degrades; no fallback direct connection or host access occurs.
verification: Disable component and rerun allowed/denied cases.
evidence: Degraded readiness, denied operations, zero sink/host counters, operator guidance.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if outage denies safely; FAIL on fallback open or misleading ready state.

given: Untrusted artifact, connector payload, web page, or tool output asks system to call URL, approve action, or change policy.
when: Content is processed by agent/workflow.
then: Content remains untrusted evidence and cannot create egress grants, action approvals, policy changes, or tool execution.
verification: Prompt-injection fixtures across artifact and connector paths.
evidence: Zero tool/action/egress calls, blocked-attempt audit, untrusted label, evidence refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if malicious content produces no authority; FAIL on content-driven authority expansion.

given: ConnectorHub delivers a projection from a read-only source.
when: Delivery process commits and acks projection.
then: CornerStone commits immutable Artifact and evidence metadata before ack; crash/retry cannot acknowledge uncommitted source truth.
verification: Projection delivery crash/retry fixture.
evidence: Artifact ID/hash/provenance, delivery attempt log, ack-after-commit proof, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if ack follows durable commit; FAIL on lost projection or ack-before-commit.

given: GitHub connector fixture is installed and used.
when: Static scan and runtime write-denial tests run.
then: Connector is read-only: zero write mappings, zero mutation commands, write attempts denied or quarantined.
verification: Static mapping scan plus runtime write-denial tests.
evidence: Capability manifest, denied write transcript, zero external mutation counters, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if no write-capable path is exposed; FAIL on mutation mapping or direct write client.

given: Connector credentials, tokens, provider payloads, or credential-bearing URLs appear in allowed/denied paths.
when: Secret canary scan runs.
then: Credentials stay in ConnectorHub; Product outputs use credential references only and redact sensitive payload fields.
verification: Secret canary scan across CLI, logs, screenshots, reports, audit, and durable state.
evidence: Zero secret scanner findings, credential-ref-only payload, redacted sink metadata.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if raw secrets never appear; FAIL on raw credential exposure.

given: Source Policy changes for connector, workspace, repo, browser scope, or capture mode.
when: Policy update, delivery/capture, revoke, and retry run.
then: SourcePolicySnapshot is tenant/workspace scoped, auditable, revocable, and enforced on next delivery/capture.
verification: Policy update -> delivery/capture -> revoke -> retry fixture.
evidence: Snapshot diff, before/after delivery decisions, revoked denial, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if source policy cannot bleed across scopes; FAIL on stale or cross-scope delivery.

given: WatchAgent/macOS/Chrome capture is enabled in local fixture mode.
when: Fixture runtime and browser/CLI checks run with pause/revoke.
then: Capture is consented, bounded, pauseable, revocable, scope-visible, and summary-only where raw capture is disallowed.
verification: Fixture runtime and browser/CLI checks with pause/revoke.
evidence: Consent record, scope config, capture summary, pause/revoke transcript, zero disallowed raw output.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if capture obeys explicit scope and stop controls; FAIL on silent or unbounded capture.
owner: AI/local plus physical-device human where needed

given: Connector delivery fails, duplicates, returns stale data, or contains prompt injection.
when: Fault injection and malicious payload fixture runs.
then: Retry/quarantine is idempotent, evidence-safe, and cannot create memory, policy, action, egress, or authority without product approval.
verification: Fault injection and malicious payload fixture.
evidence: Retry/quarantine record, zero unauthorized side effects, evidence refs, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if failed/malicious delivery remains contained; FAIL on duplicate truth or side effect.

given: Local sample tool package is built.
when: Build and manifest/schema verification run.
then: Package contains manifest, capabilities, file/network/env/model grants, ConnectorHub requirements, risk, version, evaluation rubric, signature, SBOM, and provenance metadata.
verification: Build and manifest/schema verification.
evidence: Package artifact, manifest JSON, signature, SBOM, schema validation, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if required metadata exists and validates; FAIL on missing grants, signature, or SBOM.

given: Tool package is registered in local trusted registry.
when: Registry positive/negative package tests run.
then: Registry accepts only trusted signed package metadata and rejects unsigned, tampered, stale, revoked, or unknown-source packages.
verification: Registry positive/negative package tests.
evidence: Acceptance record, rejection transcripts, signature verification output, registry audit.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if tampered/untrusted packages are rejected; FAIL on silent trust or unsigned install.

given: Pack/tool is installed but not activated.
when: Installed-inactive execution attempts run across surfaces.
then: Install makes package available but gives no mission/workspace authority, connector access, model route, egress, file/env grant, or action capability.
verification: Installed-inactive execution attempts across surfaces.
evidence: Install record, denied execution transcripts, zero side effects, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if installed package cannot act; FAIL on install-as-activation.

given: Namespace owner activates a pack/tool for workspace or mission.
when: Activation dry-run, approval, execution, and revoke test run.
then: Activation grants are explicit, least-privilege, pinned, reversible, auditable, and tied to policy decision and RequestContext.
verification: Activation dry-run, approval, execution, revoke test.
evidence: Grant record, policy decision, audit refs, capability list, revocation transcript.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if only granted capabilities work; FAIL on ungranted capability or hidden activation.

given: Activated tool attempts undeclared file, env, network, shell, model, connector, or memory access.
when: Runtime sandbox negative suite runs.
then: Sandbox denies undeclared access and records negative evidence without leaking secrets.
verification: Runtime sandbox negative suite.
evidence: Denied records, zero file/env/network/process access counters, audit refs, secret scan.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if every undeclared access is denied; FAIL on sandbox bypass.

given: Pack update is available.

then: Update shows diff of role contract, capabilities, playbooks, model policy, connector requirements, risk, evaluation results, migration notes, and rollback path before activation.
verification: Update dry-run and evaluation gate tests.
evidence: Diff artifact, evaluation result, blocked/approved update transcript, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if behavior-changing update cannot silently activate; FAIL on unreviewed update.

given: Active pack is rolled back or emergency-patched.

then: Rollback returns to previous pinned version; emergency patch is policy-governed, audited, and does not expand authority without review.
verification: Rollback and emergency-patch simulation.
evidence: Previous/new version records, rollback transcript, policy decision, affected mission list.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if rollback is deterministic and auditable; FAIL on irreversible or authority-expanding patch.

given: Operator opens VS3 status.
when: Fault-injection status tests and CLI/API/UI comparison run.
then: Status distinguishes Postgres/RLS, OPA, egress, ConnectorHub, tool runtime, registry, audit, backup/restore, migration, and human gates.
verification: Fault-injection status tests and CLI/API/UI comparison.
evidence: observe status JSON, UI/DOM snapshot, component fault results, audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if degraded components are visible and scoped; FAIL on misleading green state.

given: Policy, RLS, egress, connector, tool, action, migration, and human-gate events occur.
when: Audit contract tests and tamper fixture run.
then: Audit records are append-only, tamper-evident, tenant-safe, queryable, and link evidence/policy/action refs.
verification: Audit contract tests and tamper fixture.
evidence: Event inventory, hash/checkpoint verification, tamper detection failure, query transcript.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if required event classes are covered; FAIL on missing or mutable critical event.

given: Human-gate package is generated for each VS3-H row.

then: Package contains scope, why AI cannot verify, required human action, expected evidence, redaction rules, release impact, and blank approval/rejection record.
verification: Generate and validate evidence packages.

pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if packages prepare evidence without marking H rows PASS; FAIL if generated package is treated as human evidence.

given: VS3 changes are applied.

verification: Rerun accepted VS0 gates.

pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if fresh VS0 reports pass; FAIL on stale report reuse or regression.

given: VS3 changes are applied over accepted VS1 ontology slice.

verification: Rerun VS1 gate and cross-scope ontology tests.
evidence: VS1 scenario report, zero auto-promotions, policy/audit refs.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if fresh VS1 report passes; FAIL on auto-promotion or cross-scope leakage.

given: Prompt or connector content claims new authority over tenant, connector, egress, tool, model, memory, policy, or action.

then: Authority remains governed by RequestContext, Mission/Agent/Pack contracts, ConnectorHub capability, and policy.
verification: Red-team fixture suite.

given: New pack, connector, model, policy, table, workflow, or tool is introduced.

verification: Coverage and audit mutation tests.
evidence: Failing omission fixture, passing corrected inventory, matrix coverage output.
pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if coverage gates detect omissions; FAIL on silent scenario disappearance.

given: Reports, README, UI, help, or release metadata describe VS3.

then: Claims do not describe local/dev proof as production, real IdP, live provider, penetration-tested, human-accepted, or migration-ready.

pass_fail_criteria: PASS only if wording is no stronger than evidence; FAIL on unqualified readiness claim.

given: New dependencies, signing tools, sandbox runtime, or registry infrastructure are proposed.

given: Real or representative production-like data, retention rules, backup tooling, and rollback authority are available.
when: Human-supervised non-destructive drill occurs.
then: Migration, backup, restore, rollback, quarantine, RLS, policy, and audit drill passes under approved data rules.
